title: I built a trading journal that tracks confluences, not just entries — launching in 6 hours. Early access is almost gone.
author: Local-Amphibian9197
selftext:
Hey everyone,

I've been trading forex and indices for a while now, and like most of you I started with spreadsheets and Notion templates to track my trades. The problem? They tell you what happened, but never *why* something works.

So I built **TradingSFX** — a trading journal designed around one question: **where does your edge actually come from?**

It's not another basic entry/exit tracker. Here's what makes it different:

**Confluence tracking** — tag conditions like liquidity swept, HTF bias aligned, session overlap, etc. Then the journal shows you which combinations of confluences actually predict winners vs. which ones you *think* matter but don't.

**Performance heatmaps** — see your P&L broken down by day × hour. Instantly spot when you're profitable and when you're overtrading.

**Strategy breakdowns with breakeven win rate** — tag setups (ICT, SMC, supply & demand, whatever you use) and see the real stats: win rate, avg RR, expectancy, and whether you're actually above breakeven.

**AI trading coach** — not generic advice. It reads your actual trade data and gives you feedback on patterns, psychology, and risk management specific to *your* journal.

**Cross-filtering everything** — filter by symbol + strategy + session + day and drill into any combination. It's like having a personal quant analyst.



🚀 **Launching in less than 6 hours.**

Early access is closing when we go live. Here's the deal for the **first 50 signups:**

→ **First month completely free**

→ **40% off all plans — for life.** Not one month. Not one year. Forever.

This disappears the moment we launch and spots are filling up fast.

**Sign up here →** [tradingsfx.com](https://www.tradingsfx.com)

Free plan is available after launch too, but the 40% lifetime discount on paid plans is only for early access.

Happy to answer any questions about the features. I built this because I needed it for my own trading — hopefully it helps yours too.
